name: Plácido Domingo
abstract: José Plácido Domingo Embil (born 21 January 1941) is a Spanish opera singer, conductor, and arts administrator. He has recorded over a hundred complete operas and is well known for his versatility, regularly performing in Italian, French, German, Spanish, English, and Russian in the most prestigious opera houses in the world.

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: I have never listened to opera in my life. I never liked opera. ... afterwards comes on Placido Domingo doing Tosca at the Met.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Derek Smith
about_quotes.evidence: in that time I worked with all these guys — Placido Domingo... And I remember Placido, with a big orchestra... I have the piano part
about_quotes.doc_title: Derek Smith interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Majesty of the Seas, May 29, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Rowles
about_quotes.evidence: I met Placido Domingo at Bradley's... I said, 'This is Placido Domingo... And he and I are gonna do an opera together.'

about_quotes.speaker: Ken Peplowski
about_quotes.evidence: It was Ella Fitzgerald, Placido Domingo, Sinatra and Benny. So he was, he knew he was in tough company there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ken Peplowski interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 10, 1997 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Burrell
about_quotes.evidence: But I felt especially honored because on the classical side they gave it to Placido Domingo.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Burrell — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
